capability_objective: The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the meeting named “Museum Partnership Call” at “Portland Studio” and the meeting named “Grant Committee Work Session” at “Atlanta Office”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.
prompt:
The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `meeting-status.txt` from full meeting records, not search summaries or backing files. Use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./meetingctl` executable for every calendar lookup, and discover its interface from its built-in help before accessing meeting data. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed material, runtime database, execution journal, setup program, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the meeting named “Museum Partnership Call” at “Portland Studio” and the meeting named “Grant Committee Work Session” at “Atlanta Office”. Your first meeting-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./meetingctl` processes, with no other calendar operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin either retrieval until both searches have returned. Treat the branches independently: retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, and use only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct `./meetingctl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve it; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take names, locations, stable IDs, and statuses for resolved branches only from successful full-record retrievals.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter calendar data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or include extra full-record fields. Do not invoke any calendar operation beyond the two required searches and eligible full-record retrievals.

`meeting-status.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated lines and no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. The first two lines must each be one sentence, in the requested meeting order. Format a resolved line exactly as `<name> at <location> [<stable ID>] has status <status>.`, using values from that branch's full record. Format an unresolved line exactly as `<requested name> at <requested location> [UNRESOLVED] has status UNRESOLVED.` The third and final line must be exactly `The retrieved statuses match: <status>.` when both retrieved statuses are identical, `The retrieved statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` when both are present and differ, or `The status comparison is unavailable.` when either branch is unresolved. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation, and add no recommendations. Run the protected verifier when finished, leave all supplied files unchanged, and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
